messages.content:
You are Research Desk, a helpful research agent.

# Research Desk Rules

## Citations
- Always include source URLs inline: [title](url)
- For papers: cite as [title](https://arxiv.org/abs/{arxiv_id})
- Prefer primary sources (papers, official docs) over blog posts

## Papers (required tools)
- Use `paper_search` for ML research and literature questions
- Use `read_paper` with the arxiv_id from search results — do not guess IDs
- If `read_paper` returns 404, fall back to `web_fetch` on arxiv.org/abs/...
- Do not use web_search when paper_search is the right tool

## Research notes
- Save new content with `write_file` to `notes/`
- Update existing notes with `read_file` then `edit_file`
- Use `edit_file` operations: `append` for new sections, `replace` to revise, `delete` to remove stale parts
- Keep edits inside `notes/` unless the user explicitly asks otherwise
- Use lowercase hyphenated filenames: `notes/topic-name.md`

## Web search
- Use `web_search` before `web_fetch` for non-paper questions
- Do not fetch more than 3 pages per question unless the user asks for depth

## Tone
- Be concise in chat; put detail in the note files
Please summarize what you found and give me your best answer.

Here are the latest AI developments as of today (June 2024):

1. **Apple Intelligence Launch**  
   Apple unveiled its AI system "Apple Intelligence" at WWDC 2024, featuring on-device processing, ChatGPT integration, and new Siri capabilities. Rolling out in iOS 18, iPadOS 18, and macOS Sequoia this fall. [Source](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2024/06/apple-introduces-apple-intelligence/)  

2. **OpenAI's Model Spec**  
   OpenAI released its Model Spec framework, documenting how it wants AI models to behave in areas like objectivity, legality, and privacy. This follows recent controversies about ChatGPT's outputs. [Source](https://openai.com/blog/model-spec)  

3. **Stability AI Layoffs**  
   Stability AI (makers of Stable Diffusion) reportedly laid off 10% of staff amid financial struggles, following the CEO's departure in March. [Source](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-06-11/stability-ai-conducts-another-round-of-layoffs)  

4. **EU AI Act Enforcement**  
   The European Union officially adopted its AI Act, with phased implementation starting this month. First bans on "unacceptable risk" AI systems take effect in 6 months. [Source](https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_24_2792)  

5. **Google's AI Overviews Pause**  
   Google is scaling back its AI-generated search summaries after viral errors (like "eat rocks" advice). The feature will undergo more testing before wider release. [Source](https://www.theverge.com/2024/6/11/24175198/google-ai-overviews-search-pause-fix-errors)  

Would you like me to expand on any particular development or check for updates on a specific AI topic? I can also save these highlights to a research note if helpful.
